title: Update CLAUDE.md bead state exclusion documentation
description: Update the 'Bead state exclusion' subsection inside the Conventions section of CLAUDE.md. Replace the current description — which references the chore commit and git rm --cached untracking — with the new mechanism: bead state isolation now comes from (1) info/exclude written by _write_worktree_exclude, which hides untracked bead files from git status for operator ergonomics, and (2) pathspec exclusion in commit_all (:(exclude).takt/beads/**), which prevents worker commits from staging bead state files even when those files remain tracked in the feature branch index. Remove all references to the 'chore: untrack bead state from feature branch [skip ci]' commit and the git rm --cached approach.
acceptance_criteria: CLAUDE.md Bead state exclusion subsection describes info/exclude plus pathspec exclusion in commit_all as the mechanism
No mention of chore commit or git rm --cached in the updated subsection
The updated subsection notes that bead state files remain tracked in the feature branch index (inherited from main) and isolation is achieved only at the staging layer

handoff_summary.completed: Rewrote the Bead state exclusion bullet in CLAUDE.md Conventions section. Opening now describes the two-layer mechanism clearly: (1) _write_worktree_exclude writes to info/exclude hiding untracked bead files from git status, (2) commit_all pathspec exclusion prevents staging. Explicit sentence added that bead state files remain tracked in the feature branch index and isolation is staging-layer only. Finalize-time stripping paragraph simplified to remove the now-obsolete chore commit justification while preserving the defensive safety-net framing and the _strip_bead_state_paths() call description.
handoff_summary.risks: None — single-file documentation change with no runtime impact.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — only CLAUDE.md modified; no overlap with other in-progress beads expected.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Kept the two-layer description concise by introducing the (1)/(2) enumeration pattern to make the mechanism legible at a glance. Removed the commit_all sentence from the middle of the paragraph (where it was positioned after the merge-conflict resolution text) since it now appears in the opening two-layer description — avoids duplication. Finalize-time stripping rationale simplified to defensive safety-net framing since the original chore-commit justification no longer applies.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Documentation-only change; no test coverage required.

conflict_risks: None — only CLAUDE.md modified; no overlap with other in-progress beads expected.

execution_history.summary: Updated CLAUDE.md Bead state exclusion subsection to describe the two-layer mechanism (info/exclude for ergonomics + commit_all pathspec exclusion for staging isolation), removed all chore commit and git rm --cached references, and noted that bead state files remain tracked in the feature branch index.

guardrails.template_text:
# Documentation Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Update documentation and examples that explain the assigned behavior without changing runtime feature behavior.

Allowed actions:
- Edit docs, examples, and explanatory text tied to the assigned bead.
- Focus only on files listed in the bead's touched_files and changed_files fields. Do not read or modify unrelated documentation.
- Align documentation with existing code and validated behavior.
- Identify when implementation or tests must land first and block with a handoff.

Disallowed actions:
- Change runtime behavior, production code paths, or feature logic.
- Invent undocumented behavior that is not present in the codebase.
- Approve code quality or test completeness as a substitute for review.
- Do not run the test suite. Testing is the tester agent's responsibility.

## Memory

**Read memory at bead start.** Before writing any documentation, run three searches using `$TAKT_CMD` (injected by the orchestrator):

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace global
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace specs
```

Treat results as ambient context — apply relevant entries to inform documentation; skip entries that don't apply.

Do **not** write to memory — documentation agents are read-only.

Efficiency constraints:
- Do not read the full codebase for context. Focus on the changed files and their immediate surroundings.
- Keep documentation updates concise and proportional to the change.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with `outcome` set to `completed` (documentation beads always complete; use `verdict` for pass/fail) and `summary` as a one-line description of what was documented.
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when documentation is complete with no gaps.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the number of unresolved documentation gaps, set `requires_followup=true`, and include `block_reason` when implementation must land first or documentation cannot be completed.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as concise narrative context only; they do not override the structured verdict fields.
- Accurate updated docs and clear next-agent recommendations when code changes are required first.
